atomicity.message: Staged payload is one atomic land-11 unit: config schema/load/merge/normalize/validate/write foundations, config show/init command, robot/help docs, and config-focused tests. The staged path list is exactly cmd/burpvalve/config_test.go, cmd/burpvalve/main.go, internal/config/config.go, and internal/config/config_test.go. Unit-12 setup/init/repair config consumption, unit-17 completion verification, help_test.go future hunks, bead metadata, attestation query, and verifier prompt surfaces are not staged.

Unknown fields are rejected and path-like fields are checked for NUL bytes before acceptance.

conditions.message: Config defaults can affect future agent behavior, so this cell is governed. The staged implementation constrains autonomy by requiring explicit force or robot confirm for writes, using a final interactive confirmation defaulting to No, and documenting that config init does not run setup/init/repair/completion/hook actions.
evidence: config init help documents that it only writes the selected config file and does not run setup, init, repair, completion install, or hook wiring; it also documents --file/--force and robot confirm=true.
Robot scope/confirm handling, noninteractive wizard gating, and final confirmation prompt all route through writeConfigInit, which rejects writes without force/confirmation.
Staged-snapshot probes confirmed non-forced config init --json and robot confirm:false both returned nonzero and did not create the target .burpvalve.json.
config init -h in the staged snapshot showed confirmation defaulting to No, no setup/init/repair/completion/hook actions, and omitted fields are preserved.
